title: The Right Way to Manage Social Media on monday.com

posts.author: WorkOSMods (O.Williams)
posts.cookedHtml:
**You probably started with monday.com as a simple calendar for your posts. Maybe you duplicated a template board, added a few date columns, and thought: *“Perfect, we’ve got this sorted.

On paper, it looks like a system. In practice, execution is still manual:

  • Images sit in Files columns, but someone still has to download and re-upload them into each platform. → Double handling.

  • Captions live in Text columns, but still need to be copied and pasted into Instagram, LinkedIn, or TikTok. → Copy-paste chaos.

  • “Publish At” dates don’t mean much, because posts only go live when someone remembers to do it manually. → Missed timing.

  • Status updates like “Done” don’t reflect reality—tasks get checked off even if nothing was published. → False visibility.

The result? Boards look organized, but the workflow falls apart at the critical last step: publishing.

The Better Way: Close the Gap

What if the same tool that helps you plan could also publish?

No more bouncing between tabs. No more version confusion.

That’s where Publisher for monday.com comes in. It transforms monday from a planning board into your social media command center.

publisher-social-platfroms-940×788 108 KB

Instead of stopping at planning, you can now:

Publish everywhere with one click → Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Threads, LinkedIn (profile & page), Pinterest, Bluesky, Mastodon and Youtube (posts, stories, reels, pins, shorts …) and more.

Create dynamic post templates → Pull text, hashtags, images, and even column values directly from your boards.

Schedule with confidence → Use monday recipes for automation, or pick your own dates. All posts show up in a unified calendar.

Keep control → Separate clients and brands by workspace, and assign clear roles (Admin, Manager, Member, Viewer).

Without ever leaving monday.com

Curious to see it in action? Install now on monday.com
